surface_or_claim: Focused site claim gate currently fails.
evidence: `node --test .\tests\site.test.js` fails one static text test because `public/index.html` contains `PHI never leaves`.
code_truth: The test gate is active and blocks the exact forbidden phrase.
risk: A deploy from this worktree would carry a regulated-data guarantee already identified as unsafe.
recommended_action: Remove or scope the phrase to customer-controlled deployment proof before release.

surface_or_claim: Homepage metadata claims a model-bearing artifact.
evidence: Meta and social descriptions say the artifact contains model, examples, evaluator, and receipts.
code_truth: `src/artifact.js` says `model.gguf` is a pointer record today and LoRA weights are future.
risk: Search snippets can overclaim even if body copy is later fixed.
recommended_action: Rewrite metadata around recipe-tier artifact proof.

surface_or_claim: Homepage social metadata claims phone/runtime portability.
evidence: OG and Twitter descriptions say the artifact runs on a laptop or phone and user data never moves.
code_truth: Native/mobile runners and model-bearing artifacts remain product gates, not current proof.
risk: Link previews can imply shipped phone/runtime support.
recommended_action: Replace with scoped local JS recipe execution wording.

surface_or_claim: Homepage keywords target regulated/compliance claims.
evidence: Keywords include healthcare and EU AI Act terms.
code_truth: Compliance research found implemented controls and legal artifacts are incomplete.
risk: SEO can attract regulated buyers with expectations the product cannot yet satisfy.
recommended_action: Use regulated keywords only with careful current-state compliance pages.

surface_or_claim: Local JSON-LD claims on-device runtime with zero egress.
evidence: Structured data lists on-device runtime and zero-egress behavior.
code_truth: Current proof is local deterministic JS recipes plus benchmark egress monitoring, not general on-device model runtime.
risk: Search engines may index machine-readable overclaims invisible in visual review.
recommended_action: Add JSON-LD claim linting and render only proof-backed features.

surface_or_claim: Local JSON-LD claims optional Sigstore co-signature.
evidence: Structured data lists optional Sigstore co-signature.
code_truth: Current receipt and artifact signatures are HMAC-based; public-key and transparency-log modes are roadmap.
risk: Security buyers can treat structured data as implemented supply-chain proof.
recommended_action: Remove until release workflow and public verifier exist.

surface_or_claim: Local JSON-LD embeds BAA and VPC offers.
evidence: Offer schema describes BAA availability and optional VPC peering.
code_truth: Prior compliance and billing audits found legal/control and entitlement evidence incomplete.
risk: Machine-readable offer data can become sales collateral without review.
recommended_action: Move regulated offers behind reviewed plan/entitlement evidence.

surface_or_claim: Homepage FAQ claims every compile is strictly better.
evidence: FAQ and JSON-LD say the K-score gate refuses regressions so each compile is strictly better.
code_truth: Tune and K-score research found gates and improvement semantics are not yet universal.
risk: Users can infer automatic learning guarantees from ordinary recompiles.
recommended_action: Say K-score can block known regressions on supplied evals, not guarantee monotonic improvement.

surface_or_claim: Homepage FAQ claims `.kolm` contains a small model.
evidence: Visible FAQ and JSON-LD say the artifact contains a small model.
code_truth: Current `.kolm` fixtures contain recipes, evals, metadata, optional slots, and HMAC receipts, not model weights.
risk: This revives the central artifact-truth gap on the highest-traffic page.
recommended_action: Use artifact anatomy language: manifest, recipes, evals, receipt, optional slots.

surface_or_claim: Compile animation claims model specialization.
evidence: Scripted homepage copy says compile specializes a model, stamps K-score, signs, and writes a patient-intake artifact.
code_truth: `src/compile.js` marks LoRA/model training as later work and current sample fixtures are recipe-tier.
risk: Animated demos can become stronger proof than static copy.
recommended_action: Generate homepage terminal examples from verified fixture commands only.

surface_or_claim: Demo output claims on-device host.
evidence: Homepage scripted output includes an on-device host marker.
code_truth: No native/mobile/on-device artifact runner proof was found; browser/offline proof is scoped and separately audited.
risk: The demo implies runtime placement that has not been verified.
recommended_action: Replace host with the actual local CLI/browser proof label or omit it.

surface_or_claim: Use-case card claims signed local model replacement.
evidence: The SaaS card says paid calls become training data and Kolm builds a signed local model that does the same job for free.
code_truth: Capture, local tune, and adapter-backed promotion are preview or target architecture.
risk: Cost-savings and model-replacement claims need benchmark and quality proof.
recommended_action: Reframe as a target workflow until capture-to-artifact proof exists.

surface_or_claim: Healthcare card uses the exact blocked regulated phrase.
evidence: The healthcare card says `PHI never leaves the network`.
code_truth: Current hosted compile/capture paths can receive and store sensitive data; customer-controlled deployment boundaries are not the default public product.
risk: This is the direct failing gate and highest legal/trust risk in the local homepage.
recommended_action: Rewrite with customer-controlled deployment caveat or remove from homepage.

surface_or_claim: Legal card implies no vendor sees privileged material.
evidence: The legal card says local inference runs on privileged material with no vendor in the loop.
code_truth: Current cloud compile, capture, hosted run, and local artifact modes have different data boundaries.
risk: A broad no-vendor claim can mislead legal buyers.
recommended_action: Scope to local artifact runtime after compile and disclose hosted steps separately.

surface_or_claim: Phone card claims zero network and airplane-mode inference.
evidence: The device card says inference runs on the phone with zero network and airplane-mode support.
code_truth: Current browser/PWA recipe-registry mode is the safest scoped proof; native model runtime is not shipped.
risk: Phone-native model inference claims are easy to falsify.
recommended_action: Use recipe-registry PWA wording or mark target architecture.

surface_or_claim: Homepage says one signed file runs on an air-gapped box.
evidence: Use-case copy says one signed file can run on laptop, phone, server, and air-gapped box.
code_truth: Artifact files are signed, but runtime target support and airgap enforcement differ by mode.
risk: Airgap language should require explicit proof and environment assumptions.
recommended_action: Tie airgap copy to benchmarked recipe-tier local CLI only, or remove it.

surface_or_claim: Homepage claims no kill switch and decade-long portability.
evidence: Artifact card says the file can run next year offline with no kill switch.
code_truth: Current artifacts rely on JS runtime/source compatibility and HMAC secrets for verification; model/runtime bundles remain future.
risk: Long-term portability is plausible only with a versioned conformance story.
recommended_action: Add runtime/conformance version caveats.

surface_or_claim: Local exact-string gate misses equivalent overclaim variants.
evidence: The gate caught one phrase but not variants such as data never moves, zero network, phone inference, VPC boundary, or on-device zero egress.
code_truth: `tests/site.test.js` uses literal string lists.
risk: Risky claims can bypass the gate by changing words.
recommended_action: Add phrase-family checks or a structured claim manifest.

surface_or_claim: Structured-data claims need their own parser gate.
evidence: JSON-LD and meta fields contain several high-risk claims.
code_truth: The current static text test sees text but does not classify metadata versus visible copy or validate proof links.
risk: Search-visible claims can drift independently from body copy.
recommended_action: Parse meta, OG, Twitter, JSON-LD, FAQ, and offers into a claim matrix in CI.

surface_or_claim: Live homepage remains on older model-artifact positioning.
evidence: Live `https://kolm.ai/` says Kolm compiles data into a model and returns an offline artifact, with multi-GB model-artifact examples and phone positioning.
code_truth: Local source truth is recipe-tier or pointer-tier for current artifacts.
risk: Live visitors still see claims that local research has marked unsafe.
recommended_action: Replace live homepage after local claim cleanup or add visible preview labels.

surface_or_claim: Live homepage still implies public-key and on-chain proof.
evidence: Live home shows public-key-looking signature language and on-chain receipt language.
code_truth: Current receipts and signatures are HMAC-based; public-key and transparency-log modes are roadmap.
risk: Security reviewers can quickly invalidate the public proof story.
recommended_action: Remove public-key/on-chain wording until a public verifier and release proof exist.

surface_or_claim: Live docs remain on the older API/package surface.
evidence: Live docs say 21 endpoints and use old install examples, old hosted API examples, and old brand framing.
code_truth: Local router and SDK/package research show a different current surface and package strategy.
risk: Developers can copy commands that are not the current contract.
recommended_action: Generate and deploy docs from the current route/package manifest.

surface_or_claim: Live/local parity is not a deployment gate yet.
evidence: Local and live pages differ materially in claims, docs, install, and product status.
code_truth: Existing local tests do not fetch live pages by default.
risk: A green local build would not prove public claims are fixed.
recommended_action: Add a live fetch scan before and after deployment.

surface_or_claim: README and package metadata also overclaim local model portability.
evidence: Root README and package description still say signed artifacts run locally with model-like framing, while a later README caveat narrows v0 contents.
code_truth: `src/artifact.js` and the README implementation note say v0 artifacts are pointer/recipe/eval/receipt data, not embedded production weights.
risk: Public package metadata and top-of-file README copy may be read before caveats.
recommended_action: Align metadata and introduction with the implementation note.

surface_or_claim: Homepage proof should be generated from verified fixtures.
evidence: Current homepage demos are handwritten around patient intake, phone, VPC, and model specialization.
code_truth: Four fixture artifacts have real end-to-end tests and benchmark assertions.
risk: Handwritten proof copy can drift faster than tests.
recommended_action: Use sample, redactor, extractor, and classifier fixture output to render homepage proof blocks.
